name: Tekkeitsertok
description: Tekkeitsertok is a significant deity in Inuit mythology, particularly revered by hunters. He is the master of caribou, responsible for their abundance and distribution. Unlike some other powerful figures in Inuit lore who might be feared, Tekkeitsertok is generally seen as benevolent, though respect for him is paramount for ensuring successful hunts. He dwells in the mountains, often envisioned as a solitary figure observing the caribou herds and the humans who depend on them. Hunters would perform rituals and offer prayers to Tekkeitsertok to ask for his favor, believing that a pleased Tekkeitsertok would guide the caribou towards them. The deity's power lies in his control over the caribou, which are a vital source of food, clothing, and tools for Inuit communities. His influence extends to the success and survival of hunters, making him a crucial figure in their spiritual beliefs. While not typically depicted in elaborate stories like some other deities, the constant need to appease him through respectful hunting practices and offerings solidified his importance in daily life. He represents the essential relationship between humans and animals, and the need for balance and respect in the natural world. Some accounts describe him as having the ability to take the form of a caribou himself, highlighting his deep connection to the animals he protects. The proper disposal of caribou bones was also crucial to avoid offending Tekkeitsertok, ensuring the continuation of successful hunts in the future.
subtitle: Master of Caribou, Provider of Sustenance

keyMyths.title: The First Caribou
keyMyths.description: Tekkeitsertok created the first caribou from stone, breathing life into them. This act established his role as the guardian of the caribou herds.

keyMyths.title: Respecting the Hunt
keyMyths.description: A hunter who disrespects the caribou or Tekkeitsertok will find the caribou herds disappearing. Proper respect and offering are essential for continued success.

keyMyths.title: Tekkeitsertok's Guidance
keyMyths.description: Shamans communicate with Tekkeitsertok to understand the movements of the caribou. This knowledge is then used to guide the hunters to plentiful hunts.

sources.author: Franz Boas
sources.description: Early anthropological study of Inuit culture and beliefs.
sources.text: Early anthropological study of Inuit culture and beliefs.

sources.author: Knud Rasmussen
sources.description: Account of travels and encounters with Inuit people and their traditions.
sources.text: Account of travels and encounters with Inuit people and their traditions.
